text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by analyzing vast amounts of text data, called training data, which includes books, articles, and websites. Through this process, they learn patterns in language, such as grammar and facts about the world. When you give an LLM a prompt, or instruction, it uses these patterns to predict the next word and build a response. This is known as prompt engineering, as carefully crafting your question can improve the answer. LLMs have impressive capabilities, like writing stories or answering questions, but they also have limits—they can sometimes produce inaccurate information or misunderstand context, especially if the prompt is unclear.
